lead_signal.headline: No new FIMI operations identified this week — see coverage notes.
lead_signal.confidence_basis: No Tier 1-3 disclosures or attributions meeting evidentiary standards for the week ending 2026-04-11.
lead_signal.note: Exhaustive search across specified Tier 1-3 sources yielded no platform disclosures, government attributions, or OSINT reports on new FIMI activity for tracked actors (RU, CN, IR, Gulf, US, IL) in the past 7 days. Closest material (e.g., Slovenia election FIMI report dated March 2026) falls outside temporal scope.

weekly_brief_narrative: No new Foreign Information Manipulation and Interference (FIMI) operations were identified across the six tracked actors (Russia, China, Iran, Gulf states, United States, Israel) for the week ending 2026-04-11. Tier 1 sources—Meta CIB reports, Google TAG, Microsoft MSTIC, EUvsDisinfo, US State Dept GEC, and government attribution statements—returned no disclosures of takedowns, labels, or formal attributions dated within the past 7 days. Tier 2 institutional monitors (Stanford IO, EU DisinfoLab, EDMO) and Tier 3 OSINT (DFRLab, ASPI ICPC, Bellingcat) similarly produced no relevant publications on fresh campaigns, platform actions, or cognitive warfare developments. Searched queries included 'coordinated inauthentic behaviour' variants, 'influence operations [actor] 2026', 'state-sponsored disinformation', and 'information manipulation [country]' filtered to post-2026-04-01 content, discarding irrelevant film-industry results. A March 2026 report on Balkan-linked FIMI targeting Slovenia's parliamentary elections (March 22, 2026) surfaced in searches but predates the weekly window; it describes cross-platform activity (Telegram, X, TikTok) with AI-generated deepfakes and saturation tactics linked to Russia/Serbia/Hungary networks, yet lacks Tier 1 confirmation and single-source attribution (MF2 flagged if considered). A separate ECFR policy brief on China's 'borrowed mouths' influencer playbook in Europe references historical patterns (e.g., 2024 Xinjiang trips) without new 2026 incidents. Actor postures remain unchanged: Russia and China sustain high AI-FIMI baselines per 2026 calibration (Doppelganger/Spamouflage evolutions), but no weekly signals. Platform enforcement absent, consistent with X/Twitter's post-2022 transparency decline (no CIB reports). Notable absences include Gulf/US/IL operations, structurally under-reported due to OSINT gaps. This quiet week aligns with post-election lulls (e.g., Slovenia) and highlights detection challenges for AI-embedded FIMI, where content generation obscures coordination without forensic Tier 1/2 backing. Coverage confirms no signal, not absence of activity.

research_coverage.x_twitter_gap_note: X/Twitter maintains zero CIB-equivalent disclosures since Q3 2022; API restrictions limit third-party monitoring, amplifying structural blind spots for all actors.
coverage_gaps: Gulf/US/IL: Persistent low-confidence attributions due to OSINT gaps
AI-FIMI: Potential undetected synthetic content without Tier 1 forensics
